rulings.Case Title: MILA POPOV VS. BRYAN KELTNER ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 30, 2024 line 1. DEFENDANT SANKAET PATHAK, SYNAPSE FINANCIAL TECH, INC. DEMURRER TO 2ND Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege standing to pursue claims on behalf of the former owner of the property during whose ownership the contractual breaches and torts have occurred (e.g. assignment of claims). Plaintiff shall also expressly allege that the claims submitted in the prior action are not the same as the claims submitted in this action (e.g. different windows, different chair, different rug, etc.). Plaintiff's declaration submitted in opposition to the demurrer is stricken in its entirety. Defendant's declaration is stricken except for the first sentence of paragraph 7: "On November 29, 2023, I had a meet and confer telephone conference with Ms. Rishina concerning the 2SAC." =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: MILA POPOV VS. BRYAN KELTNER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Strike Improper, Irrelevant, And Confusing Matters
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 30, 2024 line 1. PLAINTIFF MILA POPOV MOTION TO STRIKE IMPROPER, IRRELEVANT, AND CONFUSING MATTERS is DENIED. Insufficient notice per CCP 1005. To the extent this motion was to serve as an objection to the defendant's request for judicial notice, the objection is overruled. The Court takes judicial notice of Court filings and attempted court filings submitted to the Court. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: HENRY CHAN ET AL VS. JUSTIN L. TIN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion & Motion To Set Aside Entry Of Default
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 30, 2024 line 2. DEFENDANT HOA COOC NOTICE OF MOTION & MOTION TO SET ASIDE ENTRY OF DEFAULT is DENIED. Moving party failed to show surprise, mistake or excusable neglect. rulings.Case Title: IAN DOMINGUEZ VS. ERNEST YEE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 30, 2024 line 3. DEFENDANT ERNEST YEE, GLORIA YEE, KIN YEE, KIN YEE, KRYSTAL YEE, RYAN YEE, STANLEY YEE MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is DENIED. Plaintiff sufficiently states facts re: conscious disregard of Plaintiff's rights. The Court notes that Plaintiff failed to address the missing request for punitive damages in the Prayer section of the Complaint. Plaintiff shall file an Amendment to the Complaint to clarify that punitive damages are prayed for as is asserted in the body of the complaint. Plaintiff shall have 10 days for file an Amendment. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
